summary: 10 concrete items surfaced from 19 qualifying stories.
telegram_footer: 10 items surfaced | 19/19 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Claude Sonnet 5
items.summary: Anthropic's agentic Sonnet model for browser, terminal, and planning workflows.
items.why_included: Commenters framed it as the cheaper, more agentic middle ground versus Opus.
items.evidence: HN commenters said Sonnet 5 can handle agentic computer use at lower cost than Opus 4.8, though some thought it over-optimizes for autonomous work.

items.name: Claude Science
items.summary: Anthropic's science-focused assistant and workspace for research and data tasks.
items.why_included: The thread highlighted its browser-based, tool-connected workflow for scientific users.
items.evidence: A commenter said the integrations with databases, computational tools, and institutional clusters are the real value, even beyond plotting and papers.

items.name: Biomni HPC
items.summary: A biomedical AI/HPC platform that gives agents access to tools, databases, and cluster jobs.
items.why_included: A connected-tool author called the integration layer the hard part and the valuable part.
items.evidence: The thread said Biomni makes tool/database/HPC integration usable for bioinformatics and can shorten the path from question to result.

items.name: Knoppix
items.summary: The classic bootable Linux live CD for rescue and tinkering.
items.why_included: The discussion was packed with first-Linux memories and recovery workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters recalled Knoppix as their first Linux system and a safe way to experiment without touching hard drives.

items.name: ZLUDA 6 release
items.source_story_title: Zluda 6 release (run unmodified CUDA applications on non-Nvidia GPUs)
items.summary: A CUDA translation layer that runs unmodified CUDA apps on non-Nvidia GPUs.
items.why_included: HN focused on the compatibility gains and the practical edge cases it unlocks.
items.evidence: Comments highlighted the new textures, PhysX, and Windows support, and debated whether ZLUDA can loosen Nvidia's CUDA lock-in.

items.name: Dyson Sphere Program
items.summary: A sci-fi factory game about scaling from a workshop to a galaxy-wide industrial empire.
items.why_included: Factorio fans recommended it as the fresher, more techno-optimistic alternative.
items.evidence: Commenters said it captures Factorio's core loop with a fresh spin and praised its scale and multiplayer support.

items.name: 1.38 Millimeter Microcontroller
items.summary: TI's tiny MSPM0C1104 microcontroller for ultra-low-cost embedded projects.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as serious jellybean silicon, not a novelty part.
items.evidence: The thread called it TI's answer to the CH32V003, Puya PY32, and STM32C0 class, while noting the tight SRAM budget.

items.name: I built a mmWave material classification radar
items.source_story_title: I built a mmWave material classification radar
items.summary: A DIY mmWave radar project that classifies materials.
items.why_included: The project drew strong interest despite skepticism about the proof-of-concept gap.
items.evidence: Commenters called it very cool and super cool, while debating whether it had actually proven the material-detection use case.

items.name: Building a custom octocopter from scratch with no prior hardware experience
items.source_story_title: Building a custom octocopter from scratch with no prior hardware experience
items.summary: A from-scratch octocopter build that documents the mechanics and trade-offs.
items.why_included: The thread got praise for the ambitious build and the design details.
items.evidence: HN commenters called it a super cool project and discussed frame materials, filament, and CNC choices.

items.name: Open Source Low Tech
items.summary: A catalog of open-source, low-tech designs for practical local fabrication.
items.why_included: Commenters liked the self-sufficiency angle and the build-it-locally mindset.
items.evidence: Discussion praised teaching communities to build and repair locally instead of depending on shipped materials.
